name: Ganesha

visual_attributes.morphology: Anthropomorphic figure with an elephant head and a human body
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Varies from small statues to large temple idols
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Typically seated or dancing, with trunk turned towards a sweet

domain_specific_visual.ICONOGRAPHY: Holds an axe or goad, a noose, a broken tusk, and a sweet

secondary_functions: Deva of logic, intellect, and wisdom

domain_specific_functional.RITUAL_ROLE: Honored at the start of rites and ceremonies
domain_specific_functional.EDUCATIONAL_ROLE: Invoked during writing sessions as a patron of letters and learning

relationships.description: Ganesha is the son of Parvati
Ganesha is the son of Shiva

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Widely revered as a deity across Hindu, Jain, and Buddhist traditions

contrastive_constraints: NOT a deity without an elephant head
